qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ExecutionStatus bypasses logs ACL and exposes action output to view-only users
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the request-response ExecutionStatus lookup path, while the advisory describes the separate EventStream subscription and broadcast callbacks leaking execution output to subscribers without per-recipient ACL checks.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's EventStream-specific unauthorized broadcast path; the accepted session finding concerns a different ExecutionStatus handler.
evidence: The advisory root cause is EventStream() admitting any dashboard-authorized subscriber and OnExecutionStarted, OnExecutionFinished, and OnOutputChunk broadcasting execution data to every connected client without a per-recipient ACL check.

The only final qualifying finding report names POST /api/ExecutionStatus as the vulnerable entrypoint and describes fetching the most recent execution output by action binding ID through a normal request-response API call.

Both issues concern unauthorized disclosure of action output, but they rely on different endpoints, different control flow, and different missing authorization checks, so the finding is not the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause.
